The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} era, two {{jalink|war banner|Q2912707|war banners}} used by {{jalink|Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{jalink|Taira clan|Q568647|Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296}}, swords by {{jalink|Yamato no Kami}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{jalink|Muramasa|Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{jalink|Toyokoku Shōshi}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{jalink|Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{jalink|Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{jalink|World War II|Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{jalink|Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{jalink|Tale of the Heike|Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{jalink|Lower shrine|Q30935472|Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:need translation]]

Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=竹田市}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Ōita Prefecture|Q133924}}{{jalink|Taketa|Q850508}} Kanbara 1822 (Lower shrine) |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Takeo Shimokorihiko Ōkami
{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970}} and 9 other deities[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319c1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Lower Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei |社格 = Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine|Q90139226}} |創建 ={{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} 2 ({{jalink|651|Q31095}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takeo Shimokorihiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine''' (たけおしもごおりひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōita Prefecture|Q133924}} {{jalink|Taketa|Q850508}}. === Overview === The shrine originates from rituals associated with the worship of {{jalink|Mount Sobo|Q3695986}}. It consists of the '''Upper shrine''' on the summit of Mount Sobo, the '''Lower shrine''' in Kanbara, Taketa, the '''Shinkōsho''' (procession site), and '''Anamori Shrine'''. It is also known as '''Ubadake Daimyōjin''', '''Uha Myōjin''', and '''Sobosan Daimyōjin'''. === History === It is said that the shrine buildings of the Lower shrine were erected in {{jalink|651|Q31095}} ({{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} 2), during the {{jalink|Taika Reform|Q570140}}. {{quote|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine
Section 1: Enshrined Deity
Located at Kanbara in Ubadake Village, {{jalink|Naoiri District|Q6964536}}, this is an ancient shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} is enshrined together with {{jalink|Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970}}, and it is called Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine. It was consecrated in {{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} 2 of {{jalink|Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980}}'s reign. ...|''{{jalink|Toyokoku Shōshi}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} According to the ''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'', the shrine received the {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}} (divine rank) of {{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605}} 10 ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', it received {{jalink|Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 7 ({{jalink|883|Q30355}}). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' of {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Bungo Province|Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}: it was designated a {{jalink|district shrine|Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), and a {{jalink|prefectural shrine|Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{jalink|1879|Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{jalink|mirror|Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{jalink|votive tablet|Q5358873|votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} era, two {{jalink|war banner|Q2912707|war banners}} used by {{jalink|Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{jalink|Taira clan|Q568647|Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296}}, swords by {{jalink|Yamato no Kami}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{jalink|Muramasa|Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{jalink|Toyokoku Shōshi}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{jalink|Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{jalink|Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{jalink|World War II|Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{jalink|Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{jalink|Tale of the Heike|Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{jalink|Lower shrine|Q30935472|Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]]

Takeo Shimokorihiko Ōkami
{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970}} and 9 other deities[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319c1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Lower Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei |社格 = Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine|Q90139226}} |創建 ={{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} 2 ({{jalink|651|Q31095}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takeo Shimokorihiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine''' (たけおしもごおりひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōita Prefecture|Q133924}} {{jalink|Taketa|Q850508}}. === Overview === The shrine originates from rituals associated with the worship of {{jalink|Mount Sobo|Q3695986}}. It consists of the '''Upper shrine''' on the summit of Mount Sobo, the '''Lower shrine''' in Kanbara, Taketa, the '''Shinkōsho''' (procession site), and '''Anamori Shrine'''. It is also known as '''Ubadake Daimyōjin''', '''Uha Myōjin''', and '''Sobosan Daimyōjin'''. === History === It is said that the shrine buildings of the Lower shrine were erected in {{jalink|651|Q31095}} ({{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} 2), during the {{jalink|Taika Reform|Q570140}}. {{quote|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine
Section 1: Enshrined Deity
Located at Kanbara in Ubadake Village, {{jalink|Naoiri District|Q6964536}}, this is an ancient shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} is enshrined together with {{jalink|Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970}}, and it is called Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine. It was consecrated in {{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} 2 of {{jalink|Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980}}'s reign. ...|''{{jalink|Toyokoku Shōshi}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} According to the ''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'', the shrine received the {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}} (divine rank) of {{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1075605}} 10 ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', it received {{jalink|Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 7 ({{jalink|883|Q30355}}). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' of {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Bungo Province|Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}: it was designated a {{jalink|district shrine|Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), and a {{jalink|prefectural shrine|Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{jalink|1879|Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{jalink|mirror|Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{jalink|votive tablet|Q5358873|votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{jalink|Hakuchi|Q540118}} era, two {{jalink|war banner|Q2912707|war banners}} used by {{jalink|Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{jalink|Taira clan|Q568647|Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296}}, swords by {{jalink|Yamato no Kami}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{jalink|Muramasa|Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{jalink|Toyokoku Shōshi}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{jalink|Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{jalink|Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{jalink|World War II|Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{jalink|Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{jalink|Tale of the Heike|Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{jalink|Lower shrine|Q30935472|Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]][[Category:qq]]

According to the ''{{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', it received {{jalink|Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 7 ({{jalink|883|Q30355}}). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{jalink|Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. {{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}} and 9 other deities[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319c1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Lower Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei |社格 = Former {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q90139226|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 ({{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takeo Shimokorihiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine''' (たけおしもごおりひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} {{ill|lt=Taketa|Q850508|WD=Q850508}}. === Overview === The shrine originates from rituals associated with the worship of {{ill|lt=Mount Sobo|Q3695986|WD=Q3695986}}. It consists of the '''Upper shrine''' on the summit of Mount Sobo, the '''Lower shrine''' in Kanbara, Taketa, the '''Shinkōsho''' (procession site), and '''Anamori Shrine'''. It is also known as '''Ubadake Daimyōjin''', '''Uha Myōjin''', and '''Sobosan Daimyōjin'''. === History === It is said that the shrine buildings of the Lower shrine were erected in {{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2), during the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|Q570140|WD=Q570140}}. {{quote|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine
Section 1: Enshrined Deity
Located at Kanbara in Ubadake Village, {{ill|lt=Naoiri District|Q6964536|WD=Q6964536}}, this is an ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} is enshrined together with {{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}}, and it is called Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine. It was consecrated in {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}}'s reign. ...|''{{ill||lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} According to the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'', the shrine received the {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} (divine rank) of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469}}). According to the ''{{ill||lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|UNKNOWN}}'', it received {{ill|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|WD=Q11123280|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Bungo Province|Q864148|WD=Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}: it was designated a {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), and a {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{ill|lt=mirror|Q35197|WD=Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{ill|lt=votive tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|lt=votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} era, two {{ill|lt=war banner|Q2912707|WD=Q2912707|lt=war banners}} used by {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{ill|lt=Taira clan|Q568647|WD=Q568647|lt=Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296|WD=Q16767296}}, swords by {{ill||lt=Yamato no Kami|UNKNOWN}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{ill|lt=Muramasa|Q1849828|WD=Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{ill||lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{ill|lt=Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{ill|lt=Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{ill|lt=Tale of the Heike|Q830740|WD=Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{ill|lt=Lower shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695|WD=Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It was consecrated in {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}}'s reign. ...|''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} According to the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'', the shrine received the {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} (divine rank) of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469}}). According to the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|UNKNOWN}}'', it received {{ill|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|WD=Q11123280|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Bungo Province|Q864148|WD=Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}: it was designated a {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), and a {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{ill|lt=mirror|Q35197|WD=Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{ill|lt=votive tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|lt=votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} era, two {{ill|lt=war banner|Q2912707|WD=Q2912707|lt=war banners}} used by {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{ill|lt=Taira clan|Q568647|WD=Q568647|lt=Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296|WD=Q16767296}}, swords by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato no Kami|UNKNOWN}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{ill|lt=Muramasa|Q1849828|WD=Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{ill|lt=Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{ill|lt=Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{ill|lt=Tale of the Heike|Q830740|WD=Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{ill|lt=Lower shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695|WD=Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}} and 9 other deities[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319c1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Lower Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei |社格 = Former {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q90139226|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 ({{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takeo Shimokorihiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine''' (たけおしもごおりひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} {{ill|lt=Taketa|Q850508|WD=Q850508}}. === Overview === The shrine originates from rituals associated with the worship of {{ill|lt=Mount Sobo|Q3695986|WD=Q3695986}}. It consists of the '''Upper shrine''' on the summit of Mount Sobo, the '''Lower shrine''' in Kanbara, Taketa, the '''Shinkōsho''' (procession site), and '''Anamori Shrine'''. It is also known as '''Ubadake Daimyōjin''', '''Uha Myōjin''', and '''Sobosan Daimyōjin'''. === History === It is said that the shrine buildings of the Lower shrine were erected in {{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2), during the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|Q570140|WD=Q570140}}. {{quote|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine
Section 1: Enshrined Deity
Located at Kanbara in Ubadake Village, {{ill|lt=Naoiri District|Q6964536|WD=Q6964536}}, this is an ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} is enshrined together with {{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}}, and it is called Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine. It was consecrated in {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}}'s reign. ...|''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} According to the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'', the shrine received the {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} (divine rank) of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469}}). According to the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it received {{ill|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|WD=Q11123280|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Bungo Province|Q864148|WD=Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}: it was designated a {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), and a {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{ill|lt=mirror|Q35197|WD=Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{ill|lt=votive tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|lt=votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} era, two {{ill|lt=war banner|Q2912707|WD=Q2912707|lt=war banners}} used by {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{ill|lt=Taira clan|Q568647|WD=Q568647|lt=Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296|WD=Q16767296}}, swords by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{ill|lt=Muramasa|Q1849828|WD=Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{ill|lt=Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{ill|lt=Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{ill|lt=Tale of the Heike|Q830740|WD=Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{ill|lt=Lower shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695|WD=Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=竹田市}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ōita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}}{{ill|lt=Taketa|Q850508|WD=Q850508}} Kanbara 1822 (Lower shrine) |位置 = {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}} and 9 other deities[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319c1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Lower Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei |社格 = Former {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q90139226|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 ({{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takeo Shimokorihiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine''' (たけおしもごおりひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} {{ill|lt=Taketa|Q850508|WD=Q850508}}. === Overview === The shrine originates from rituals associated with the worship of {{ill|lt=Mount Sobo|Q3695986|WD=Q3695986}}. It consists of the '''Upper shrine''' on the summit of Mount Sobo, the '''Lower shrine''' in Kanbara, Taketa, the '''Shinkōsho''' (procession site), and '''Anamori Shrine'''. It is also known as '''Ubadake Daimyōjin''', '''Uha Myōjin''', and '''Sobosan Daimyōjin'''. === History === It is said that the shrine buildings of the Lower shrine were erected in {{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2), during the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|Q570140|WD=Q570140}}. {{quote|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine
Section 1: Enshrined Deity
Located at Kanbara in Ubadake Village, {{ill|lt=Naoiri District|Q6964536|WD=Q6964536}}, this is an ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} is enshrined together with {{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}}, and it is called Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine. It was consecrated in {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}}'s reign. ...|''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} According to the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'', the shrine received the {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} (divine rank) of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469}}). According to the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it received {{ill|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|WD=Q11123280|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Bungo Province|Q864148|WD=Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}: it was designated a {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), and a {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{ill|lt=mirror|Q35197|WD=Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{ill|lt=votive tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|lt=votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} era, two {{ill|lt=war banner|Q2912707|WD=Q2912707|lt=war banners}} used by {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{ill|lt=Taira clan|Q568647|WD=Q568647|lt=Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296|WD=Q16767296}}, swords by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{ill|lt=Muramasa|Q1849828|WD=Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{ill|lt=Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{ill|lt=Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{ill|lt=Tale of the Heike|Q830740|WD=Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{ill|lt=Lower shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695|WD=Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Bungo Province|Q864148|WD=Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}: it was designated a {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), and a {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{ill|lt=mirror|Q35197|WD=Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{ill|lt=votive tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|lt=votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} era, two {{ill|lt=war banner|Q2912707|WD=Q2912707|lt=war banners}} used by {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{ill|lt=Taira clan|Q568647|WD=Q568647|lt=Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296|WD=Q16767296}}, swords by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{ill|lt=Muramasa|Q1849828|WD=Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{ill|lt=Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{ill|lt=Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{ill|lt=Tale of the Heike|Q830740|WD=Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{ill|lt=Lower shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695|WD=Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}} and 9 other deities[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319c1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Lower Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei |社格 = Former {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q90139226|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 ({{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takeo Shimokorihiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine''' (たけおしもごおりひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} {{ill|lt=Taketa|Q850508|WD=Q850508}}. === Overview === The shrine originates from rituals associated with the worship of {{ill|lt=Mount Sobo|Q3695986|WD=Q3695986}}. It consists of the '''Upper shrine''' on the summit of Mount Sobo, the '''Lower shrine''' in Kanbara, Taketa, the '''Shinkōsho''' (procession site), and '''Anamori Shrine'''. It is also known as '''Ubadake Daimyōjin''', '''Uha Myōjin''', and '''Sobosan Daimyōjin'''. === History === It is said that the shrine buildings of the Lower shrine were erected in {{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2), during the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|Q570140|WD=Q570140}}. {{quote|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine
Section 1: Enshrined Deity
Located at Kanbara in Ubadake Village, {{ill|lt=Naoiri District|Q6964536|WD=Q6964536}}, this is an ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} is enshrined together with {{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}}, and it is called Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine. It was consecrated in {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}}'s reign. ...|''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} According to the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'', the shrine received the {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} (divine rank) of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469}}). According to the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it received {{ill|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|WD=Q11123280|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Bungo Province|Q864148|WD=Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}: it was designated a {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), and a {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{ill|lt=mirror|Q35197|WD=Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{ill|lt=votive tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|lt=votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} era, two {{ill|lt=war banner|Q2912707|WD=Q2912707|lt=war banners}} used by {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{ill|lt=Taira clan|Q568647|WD=Q568647|lt=Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296|WD=Q16767296}}, swords by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{ill|lt=Muramasa|Q1849828|WD=Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{ill|lt=Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{ill|lt=Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{ill|lt=Tale of the Heike|Q830740|WD=Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{ill|lt=Lower shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695|WD=Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Takeo Shimokorihiko Ōkami
{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}} and 9 other deities[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319c1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Lower Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei |社格 = Former {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q90139226|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 ({{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takeo Shimokorihiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine''' (たけおしもごおりひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} {{ill|lt=Taketa|Q850508|WD=Q850508}}. === Overview === The shrine originates from rituals associated with the worship of {{ill|lt=Mount Sobo|Q3695986|WD=Q3695986}}. It consists of the '''Upper shrine''' on the summit of Mount Sobo, the '''Lower shrine''' in Kanbara, Taketa, the '''Shinkōsho''' (procession site), and '''Anamori Shrine'''. It is also known as '''Ubadake Daimyōjin''', '''Uha Myōjin''', and '''Sobosan Daimyōjin'''. === History === It is said that the shrine buildings of the Lower shrine were erected in {{ill|lt=651|Q31095|WD=Q31095}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2), during the {{ill|lt=Taika Reform|Q570140|WD=Q570140}}. {{quote|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine
Section 1: Enshrined Deity
Located at Kanbara in Ubadake Village, {{ill|lt=Naoiri District|Q6964536|WD=Q6964536}}, this is an ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} is enshrined together with {{ill|lt=Itsuse no Mikoto|Q10881970|WD=Q10881970}}, and it is called Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine. It was consecrated in {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} 2 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}}'s reign. ...|''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} According to the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'', the shrine received the {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} (divine rank) of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469}}). According to the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it received {{ill|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|Q11123280|WD=Q11123280|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7 ({{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" is listed as one of the five Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Bungo Province|Q864148|WD=Q864148}}. Either the Lower shrine or Anamori Shrine of this shrine is identified as the shrine in question. Regarding the former shrine ranking under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}: it was designated a {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), and a {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}). === Shrine treasures === The treasures include a {{ill|lt=mirror|Q35197|WD=Q35197}}, sacred swords, {{ill|lt=votive tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|lt=votive tablets}}, war banners, and others. {{quote| Section 2: Shrine Treasures
The shrine storehouse contains timbers from the {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|WD=Q540118}} era, two {{ill|lt=war banner|Q2912707|WD=Q2912707|lt=war banners}} used by {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} in the pursuit of the {{ill|lt=Taira clan|Q568647|WD=Q568647|lt=Taira clan}}, two votive tablets, a mirror by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Mitsunaga|Q16767296|WD=Q16767296}}, swords by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} Tadayuki, Rai Yukinaga, Ise {{ill|lt=Muramasa|Q1849828|WD=Q1849828}}, Ryōkai, Amakuni, and over a dozen other unsigned sacred swords, as well as wooden dogs, crocodile figures, and other items.{{efn|Mokuku (wooden dogs) are animals resembling weasels (''[https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/show/C0078820 Illustrations of Various Beasts]'', "[https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%9C%A8%E7%8B%97 Baidu Encyclopedia]").}} |''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokoku Shōshi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (1907){{sfn|大分県|1907}}}} === Overview of sub-shrines === === Upper shrine === This is a small shrine located on the summit of Mount Sobo. At present, no rituals are conducted at this location. Note that the summit of Mount Sobo also has an upper shrine belonging to Sobodake Shrine, which is located at the foot of the mountain on the Miyazaki Prefecture side. === Lower shrine === * Location: 1822 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture It is said that the shrine buildings were erected in Hakuchi 2 (650). It is also called '''Takeo Shrine''' or '''Takeo-sha'''. Together with {{ill|lt=Anamori Shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Anamori Shrine}}, it is considered a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Shinkōsho (procession site) === * Location: 1772 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture This was formerly a place of distant worship (yōhaijo). In Shōwa 9 ({{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}}), shrine buildings were erected with the aim of elevating the shrine to the rank of {{ill|lt=Kokuhei Shōsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} (minor national shrine), but this promotion was never achieved. After {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, the site became the center of shrine rituals as the Shinkōsho[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319b1.html Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine Shinkōsho, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. === Anamori Shrine === * Location: 1432 Ōaza Kanbara, Taketa, Ōita Prefecture * Enshrined deity: Ubadake Daimyōjin Behind the shrine there is a cave where the great serpent, believed to be the incarnation of Ubadake Daimyōjin, is said to have dwelt. This cave is regarded as the sacred object of worship (goshintai). The birth legend of {{ill|lt=Ogata Koreyoshi|Q7079849|WD=Q7079849}} associated with this great serpent is also recounted in the ''{{ill|lt=Tale of the Heike|Q830740|WD=Q830740}}'', Book Eight, in the chapter titled "Onokami" (Ogata's Lineage)[http://www.manabu-oshieru.com/daigakujuken/kobun/heike/08/0303.html Tale of the Heike - Book Eight, Onokami: "In the province of Bungo..." (Original text and modern translation)] Manabu-Oshieru.COM. There is a tradition that taking home a small stone from inside the cave will bring the blessing of children, and many visitors come to pray for success in romance and fertility[https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/photo_news/?id=971 Photo News: The torii gate on the approach to Anamori Shrine restored to its beautiful form for the first time in 50 years!] Taketa City, August 30, 2014. The shrine was formerly also called '''Ike no Myōjin''' (Pond Deity) or '''Iwaya Daimyōjin''' (Cave Great Deity)[http://www.niigata-u.com/files/oita2017/170319a1.html Anamori Shrine, Kanbara, Taketa City] Ōita Hyakkei. Together with the {{ill|lt=Lower shrine|Q30935472|WD=Q30935472|lt=Lower shrine}}, it is considered a candidate shrine for "Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine" listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō''. === Notes === ;Annotations {{notelist}} ;Sources {{reflist|2}} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Bungo Province|Q11633695|WD=Q11633695}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県|authorlink=大分県| translator=| title=[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/766788/82 豊国小志]| page=| publisher=甲斐書店| location =大分県| year=1907| isbn=| ref=harv}} * 歴史的行政区域データセットβ版『[https://geoshape.ex.nii.ac.jp/city/resource/44B0120020.html 直入郡嫗嶽村]』 {{DEFAULTSORT:たけおしもこりひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeo Shimokorihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:竹田市の歴史]] [[Category:竹田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社|小たけおしもこりひこしんしや]] [[Category:竹田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:大分県の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:神社の画像提供依頼]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
